title: Lesson plan ✎ Very rough draft

markdown.headingText: Lesson plan ✎ Very rough draft
markdown.markdown: - badge 1 ✎ Very rough draft - badge 2 ✎ Rough draft - badge 3 ✎ Polishing - badge 4 ✓ Complete If delivered as a semester-long course, we suggest the following lesson plan. Notes to self on structure - part: "Part I: Learning to Simulate" chapters: - chapters/1_required_skills.qmd # week 1 homework - chapters/2_foundation_concepts.qmd # week 1 ian - chapters/3_writing_functions.qmd # week 1 jamie - chapters/4_data_generating_process_functions.qmd # week 2 - really about practicing writing functions, summarizing and plotting - ian - chapters/5_analysis_functions.qmd # week 3 - practice writing functions again, more/less flexible/reusable - jamie - chapters/6_creating_simulation_experiments.qmd # week 4, if it includes Siepe's study on designs - chapters/7_mapping.qmd # week 4 - nested dfs needs explaining. - chapters/8_summarizing_results.qmd # week 5 - chapters/9_planning_and_reporting.qmd # come back to this after substantive chapter 1 - week 7?, Siepe paper, assessing performance (MC SE), etc. Diagnostics on already run simulations, go in and check specific iterations, run manually etc. # maybe add sth here on parallelisation and optimisation, eg dropping the nested datasets and using a helper function to use pmap() more directly and efficiently? - chapters/10_simulation_template.qmd # week 5, 'putting it all together'; skip to substantive chapter 1? - part: "Part II: Learning from Simulations" chapters: # whatever chapter comes next, lay out the final assignment here # substantive chapter 1 - week 5/6 - chapters/violating_assumptions.qmd ## Week 1 In class - Slides in [keynote](https://github.com/ianhussey/improving-your-statistical-inferences-through-monte-carlo-simulations/raw/main/slides/week1/simulation_week_1.key), [powerpoint](https://github.com/ianhussey/improving-your-statistical-inferences-through-monte-carlo-simulations/raw/main/slides/week1/simulation_week_1.pptx), or [pdf](https://github.com/ianhussey/improving-your-statistical-inferences-through-monte-carlo-simulations/raw/main/slides/week1/simulation_week_1.pdf). After class - [Chapter 1: Required skills](../chapters/1_required_skills.qmd) and its exercises. - [Chapter 2: Foundation concepts](../chapters/2_foundation_concepts.qmd). ## Week 2 In class - [Chapter 3: Writing functions](../chapters/3_writing_functions.qmd). After class - Chapter 3's exercises (linked in chapter). ## Week 3 In class - [Slides: Is this normally distributed?](https://github.com/ianhussey/improving-your-statistical-inferences-through-monte-carlo-simulations/blob/main/slides/week3/is_this_normally_distributed.html). - [Chapter 4: Data Generating Process functions](../chapters/4_data_generating_process_functions.qmd). After class - Chapter 4's exercises (linked in chapter). - [Chapter 5: Pseudo-random number generators](../chapters/5_pseudo_random_number_generators.qmd). ## Week 4 In class - [Chapter 6: Analysis functions](../chapters/5_analysis_functions.qmd). - [Chapter 11: Everything is regression](../chapters/11_everything_is_regression.qmd). After class - Chapter 5's exercises (linked in chapter). ## Week 5 In class - [Chapter 7: Creating simulation experiments](../chapters/6_creating_simulation_experiments.qmd). - [Chapter 8: Mapping functions](../chapters/7_mapping.qmd). After class - Chapter 7's exercises (linked in chapter). - Chapter 8's exercises (linked in chapter). ## Week 6 In class - [Chapter 8: Summarizing results](../chapters/8_summarizing_results.qmd). - [Chapter 10: Summarizing results](../chapters/10_simulation_template.qmd). After class - XX ## Week 7 In class - XX After class - XX ## Week 8 In class - XX After class - XX ## Week 9 In class - XX After class - XX ## Week 10 In class - XX After class - XX ## Week 11 In class - XX After class - XX ## Week 12 In class - XX After class - XX ## Week 13 In class - XX After class - XX ## Week 14 In class - XX After class - XX

  .kable-table table { width: 70%; margin: 0 auto; }

    display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.3em;
    padding: 0.2em 0.65em; border-radius: 20px;
    font-size: 0.72em; font-weight: 500; font-family: monospace;
    vertical-align: middle; position: relative; top: -0.1em;
  }
  .badge-draft1   { background: #fdecea; color: #8b2a2a; border: 1px solid #f5b8b8; }
  .badge-draft2   { background: #fef0e6; color: #8b4a1a; border: 1px solid #f5cfa0; }
  .badge-draft3   { background: #fef9e7; color: #7a5c00; border: 1px solid #f5d97a; }
  .badge-complete { background: #e6f4ea; color: #1e6e3a; border: 1px solid #b7dfbe; }
</style>
